title: trematolobelia macrostachys var. kaalae
intro: Trematolobelia macrostachys var. kaalae, also known as Kaʻalaʻama, is a rare and endangered plant species endemic to the island of Oahu in Hawaii. It belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. Kaʻalaʻama can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its herbal extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function.
